title: Extract lock-recovery helpers on ConcurrentEvalAdapter
analysis: TASK ALREADY COMPLETE ON MAIN. The 5 private helper methods (read_values, write_values, read_snapshot_values, write_snapshot_values, lock_results) already exist on lines 92-133 of concurrent_eval.rs and are used throughout: values(), take_results(), build_result_shared(), and the AsyncNodeEvaluator::evaluate() impl. The git log shows these were introduced via commits 64236102 ('add 5 lock helper methods'), 87a19b99 ('refactor take_results/build_result_shared'), d02a193b ('refactor evaluate()'), and ad4e84d2 ('add tracing::warn! to into_result()'). The 6 remaining inline patterns in into_result() (lines 200-241) are intentionally kept inline because self is consumed by Arc::try_unwrap — the &self helper methods cannot be called after self is moved. This is documented in the module-level doc comment (lines 16-18). 17 poison recovery tests exist and pass (3 test modules: poison_recovery, poison_panics, poison_evaluate), all gated behind feature = 'test-utils'. The CliToolContext::lock_state() precedent pattern (crates/reify-cli/src/mcp_context.rs:55-58) uses the same unwrap_or_else(|poisoned| poisoned.into_inner()) idiom but without tracing::warn! — the ConcurrentEvalAdapter helpers enhance this pattern by adding tracing diagnostics. No code changes are required. Verification only.

prerequisites.description: Verify all 17 poison recovery tests pass with 'cargo test -p reify-runtime --test concurrent_eval --features test-utils -- poison'

Verify no remaining extractable inline unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) patterns exist outside helper methods and into_result() — grep confirms only helper definitions (5) and into_result() consumed-self paths (6) remain

steps.description: Verify existing tests confirm all 5 helpers work: run full concurrent_eval test suite including poison_recovery, poison_panics, and poison_evaluate modules. All 17 tests should pass. No new tests needed — coverage is comprehensive.

No implementation needed — mark task as done. The 5 helpers (read_values, write_values, read_snapshot_values, write_snapshot_values, lock_results) exist and replace all extractable inline occurrences. The 6 into_result() inline patterns are intentionally kept inline (self consumed by Arc::try_unwrap) and are documented.

design_decisions.decision: The 6 inline unwrap_or_else patterns in into_result() are intentionally NOT extracted into helpers
design_decisions.rationale: into_result() consumes self via Arc::try_unwrap. The &self helper methods (read_values, etc.) cannot be called after self.values/self.snapshot_values/self.results are moved out. The two code paths per field (Ok: RwLock/Mutex::into_inner poison recovery, Err: .read()/.lock() shared-ref fallback) each need their own recovery. This is documented in the module-level doc comment (lines 16-18) and the into_result() method doc (lines 191-193).

design_decisions.decision: Helpers add tracing::warn! on poison recovery, enhancing the CliToolContext::lock_state() precedent
design_decisions.rationale: CliToolContext::lock_state() silently recovers via unwrap_or_else(|poisoned| poisoned.into_inner()). ConcurrentEvalAdapter helpers emit tracing::warn! because concurrent evaluation poisoning indicates a task panic mid-computation — this diagnostic is critical for debugging production scheduler issues.

design_decisions.decision: Task is already complete on main — no code changes required
design_decisions.rationale: Git log shows commits 64236102, 87a19b99, d02a193b, ad4e84d2 implemented all requested changes. 17 tests pass. The worktree has zero diff from main.

reuse.how: Same unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) idiom used as the base pattern, enhanced with tracing::warn! in ConcurrentEvalAdapter helpers
Same inline pattern as into_result() — candidate for similar helper extraction in a separate task
poison_values(), poison_snapshot_values(), poison_results() enable targeted lock poisoning for test assertions
